description: shadcn-registry deps pinned by URL + commit SHA + license. Phase 2 (task #66) audit complete — all 7 libraries verified, licenses confirmed.

cult-ui.rationale: Marketing UI tier ships 129 premium blocks + 18 starter templates — closest fit to FGA niche marketing sites. Free MIT-licensed core remains the substrate; Cult Pro premium tier is optional and NOT depended on.
cult-ui.notes: Apex domain redirects 308 -> www. Always use www in install commands. Component names verified against live registry.json (157 entries).

aceternity.rationale: 200+ animated React components — the canonical motion library. Components allowed in unlimited paid client end-products per license terms; redistribution of source forbidden.
aceternity.notes: Proprietary license, but explicitly permits use in client end-products (sold, licensed, freely distributed). Source repo has no LICENSE file — terms live at https://ui.aceternity.com/licence. URL spelling is 'aceternity' (verified). Phase 1 manifest had typo 'acternity' — corrected.

skiper-ui.rationale: 73+ uncommon shadcn components by Gxuri (Gurvinder Singh). Component slugs are numbered (skiper1..skiper73). 24+ free + 54+ premium tier. Free tier only is used in default FGA pipeline.
skiper-ui.notes: Live registry endpoint is /registry/ (verified 200), NOT /r/ (Phase 1 manifest had wrong path — corrected). Public source mirror is legacy-skiper-ui (MIT); the live production site builds from a non-public fork. SHA tracks the public mirror. Premium components require purchase; FGA pipeline only installs free skiperN slugs.

watermelon.rationale: 963 production-ready components, React 19 + Tailwind v4 + Framer Motion. Bento, testimonial, marquee, hero1, feature variants all present. Strongest fallback when cult-ui lacks a named-section component.
watermelon.notes: PHASE 2 EXIT GATE — PASSED. MIT license confirmed via GitHub repo LICENSE file + spdx_id API. Registry URL is registry.watermelon.sh (subdomain), NOT ui.watermelon.sh/r as Phase 1 manifest had — corrected. Live registry verified to expose 963 component entries.

styleui.rationale: Whole-section templates — notio (hero + features + pricing + footer), axis (hero + features + tools + pricing + testimonials + footer). Used for section-template recipe.
styleui.notes: PHASE 2 EXIT GATE — PASSED. MIT license confirmed via GitHub repo + spdx_id API. Registry exposes 4 entries: animated-list, counter, notio, axis.

componentry.rationale: 40+ free copy-paste components by Harsh Jadhav — magnet lines, magnetic dock, scroll velocity, dither gradient.
componentry.notes: Componentry code itself is MIT, but two components (image-trail, layered-stack) depend on GSAP and inherit GSAP's commercial-use restrictions. FGA default install MUST NOT pull image-trail or layered-stack without a separate GSAP commercial license review.

dotmatrix.rationale: 55+ dot-matrix loaders organized into square / circular / triangle / hex families (20 / 20 / 20 / 10). Perfect for AI-thinking / form-submit states.
dotmatrix.notes: Custom proprietary license (NOT MIT despite open-source feel). Permits use in commercial and non-commercial products. Prohibits: redistributing components as a standalone library, sublicensing, reselling. GitHub spdx_id is NOASSERTION. FGA usage (install into client end-products only) is permitted. Component slugs use 'dotm-<shape>-<n>' format — e.g. dotm-square-2 displays as 'Pulse Ladder'.
